highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.218 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release moves `/code-review` to run as a background subagent, meaning code reviews no longer clutter your main conversation thread. This is a big workflow improvement for developers who stack multiple slash commands, as reviews now run independently without blocking your flow.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.216 Released
highlights.summary: This release fixes a significant performance bug where long sessions would slow down quadratically — if your sessions felt sluggish after many turns, this update should restore snappy response times. It also adds a `sandbox.filesystem.disabled` setting for developers who want network egress control without filesystem isolation.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.215: Manual Control Over /verify and /code-review
highlights.summary: Claude will no longer automatically trigger `/verify` or `/code-review` on its own — you must now invoke them explicitly with the slash command. This gives developers more control over when these potentially token-heavy skills run during a session.

highlights.title: You're Not Behind (Yet): Master Claude Code in 31 Minutes
highlights.summary: A concise video tutorial designed to get developers up to speed with Claude Code quickly, covering key workflows without overwhelming beginners. If you've been putting off learning Claude Code, this 31-minute watch is a practical starting point before the ecosystem evolves further.

highlights.title: Claude Code: Anatomy of a Misfeature
highlights.summary: A critical opinion piece dissecting a specific Claude Code feature that the author argues causes more harm than good, with a detailed breakdown of the design decisions involved. Worth reading for developers who want a nuanced, skeptical perspective on AI coding assistant UX rather than just enthusiast takes.

highlights.title: AI Coding Assistant Strategy Slashes Token Bills
highlights.summary: This IEEE Spectrum article explores strategies used to dramatically reduce token costs when working with AI coding assistants like Claude Code. Practical cost-management techniques are especially valuable for teams or individuals on metered API plans who want to scale usage without runaway bills.

highlights.title: Setting Up Your Spare Mac for Claude Code to Control
highlights.summary: A step-by-step guide for giving Claude Code full control of a dedicated Mac, enabling powerful computer-use automation workflows without risking your primary machine. This is a practical safety-first approach for developers who want to experiment with agentic Claude Code setups.

highlights.title: Cognikernel: Local Memory for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Cognikernel is a community-built tool that gives Claude Code persistent local memory across sessions, so architectural decisions and context from past sessions are not lost each time you start fresh. It aims to reduce reliance on manually maintaining CLAUDE.md files by automatically storing and retrieving session knowledge.

tips_of_the_day: Now that `/code-review` runs as a background subagent in v2.1.218, try stacking it at the end of a multi-step slash command sequence — your review will complete in the background while you continue working in the main conversation.

If your Claude Code sessions feel slow after dozens of turns, update to v2.1.216 immediately — a quadratic performance bug in message normalization was the likely culprit, and the fix can restore multi-second response time savings in long sessions.

Keep a short CLAUDE.md file at the root of every project with key architectural decisions, preferred libraries, and coding conventions — Claude Code reads it at session start and it acts as free, always-available memory without extra tooling.
